blocking_issues.body:
[review] slice-status-plane-verifier-missing
Slice goal is STATUS_PLANE drift verifier coverage, but the only diff vs `main` is `controller/app.py` (account model/backoff logic).; No verifier/test artifact changes were made for `STATUS_PLANE.generated.yaml` readiness/deployment semantics.
Expected fix: Implement the requested STATUS_PLANE verifier coverage (and corresponding tests) that fails on drift from live readiness/deployment semantics.

[correctness] model-capability-fallback-regression
`effective_allowed_models_for_account` now returns `allowed_models` on no-overlap instead of capability-derived models.; Call sites (e.g. account/model selection and local review model selection) use that result directly and do not enforce capability membership afterward, so stale/configured `allowed_models` can select models not in live capability probes.
Expected fix: Reintroduce capability-safe fallback behavior (or explicit post-check against capability models) so selected models cannot bypass live capability constraints; add unit tests for overlap/no-overlap cases across auth kinds.

jury_feedback_history.summary: Current delta is off-slice and introduces untested account-model selection behavior that can drift from live capability semantics.

Reviewed the slice diff against `main` for regressions and missing tests; the new status-plane verifier is wired into Fleet verify, enforces readiness/deployment semantic parity from live admin status, and includes focused pass/fail/missing-artifact coverage with no actionable defects found.

queue.title: Make fleet trust and status publication strictly live-truth so stale aggregate snapshots, poisoned completion receipts, and misleading ETA/currentness states cannot survive into post-client operations.
Extract Chummer-specific shard topology, focus heuristics, proof paths, and owner-group routing out of launcher logic into explicit project configuration contracts.
Add steady-state fleet operating profiles (`maintenance`, `standard`, `burst`) with explicit shard caps, resource budgets, and safe promotion-demotion rules.
Promote executable gates, parity proofs, and flagship-readiness materializers into first-class orchestrated jobs with freshness windows, retries, and dependency edges.
Build a compact operator surface for shard mix, queue health, proof freshness, account health, resource pressure, and blocked milestones.
Consolidate restart-safe fleet runtime configuration and validate reboot recovery behavior so policy survives supervisor restarts and host reboots without tribal knowledge.

supervisor_contract.purpose: Keep Chummer-specific supervisor topology, focus routing, and proof paths in project config instead of launcher-only code or env folklore.

burst.demotion_rule: drop back to standard on warning memory pressure, repeated high-demand provider errors, or queue contraction
burst.description: Aggressive capacity mode for broad closeout/successor waves when memory and credits are healthy.
burst.promotion_rule: enter only when status is unblocked, memory is ok, and active queue breadth exceeds standard capacity

maintenance.demotion_rule: demote here when memory is critical, credits are guarded, or external providers are unstable
maintenance.description: Conservative keepalive mode for proof refresh, unblock checks, and low-pressure maintenance.
maintenance.promotion_rule: promote only when host memory is ok and the operator explicitly selects standard or burst

standard.demotion_rule: demote to maintenance when memory pressure or provider instability persists across OODA passes
standard.description: Default steady-state mode for closeout plus successor-wave work without exhausting host headroom.
standard.promotion_rule: promote to burst only with healthy memory and enough open frontier to keep the wider fleet productive

blocking_issues.body:
[review] mirror-audit-row-not-bounded-to-stale-set
`scripts/ai/verify_design_mirror.py:108-122` only checks that `source_items` is non-empty, rooted under `/.codex-design/`, and free of `..`; it never checks that the queue row actually covers the stale mirror files it is supposed to bound.; Running `python3 scripts/ai/verify_design_mirror.py` on this branch returns `stale_paths=7 queue_errors=0`, with stale files including `.codex-design/product/README.md`, `VISION.md`, `USER_JOURNEYS.md`, and `RELEASE_PIPELINE.md`.; The published queue row at `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml:9-12` lists only `NEXT_90_DAY_PRODUCT_ADVANCE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `NEXT_90_DAY_QUEUE_STAGING.generated.yaml`, and `horizons/karma-forge.md`, so the verifier currently accepts a row that does not describe the full stale bundle.
Expected fix: Tighten the verifier so the single `audit-task-11707` row is validated against the actual stale mirror bundle it is standing in for, or regenerate the row so its bounded scope matches the stale files the verifier detects.

[tests] missing-stale-row-regression-tests
`Chummer.CoreEngine.Tests/Program.cs:5980-6045` only covers the clean-mirror/no-row case and the unexpected-leftover-file failure case.; There is no test that proves the intended new contract for this slice: stale mirror bytes plus exactly one bounded `audit-task-11707` queue row should be the only acceptable in-progress state.; There is also no test for the repeat-churn failure modes this change is meant to prevent, such as duplicate `audit-task-11707` rows or a malformed bounded row that the verifier should reject.
Expected fix: Add regression tests that exercise `verify_design_mirror.py` for the stale-with-single-row success path and for duplicate/misaligned queue-row failures.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The mirror-drift guard is close, but it does not actually enforce that the published `audit-task-11707` queue slice matches the stale mirror bundle, and the new verifier path is missing regression coverage for the intended stale-with-one-bounded-row behavior.

Every attempted read command failed before execution with: "bwrap: No permissions to create a new namespace...".; Unable to load required review inputs or run branch diff against main, so contract/compatibility/state checks were not performable.
Expected fix: Restore read-only shell/file access (or provide equivalent file and diff artifacts) and rerun round 1 review.

jury_feedback_history.summary: Local fallback review could not be executed because the runtime cannot read repository files or git state due a sandbox namespace failure.

non_blocking_issues.body:
[contracts] queue-duplicate-mixed-entry-11709
The same design-mirror task appears twice: once as a plain string entry and again as a structured object (`title/task/package_id`) in the same `items` list.; This introduces mixed entry shapes for one logical task and can lead to duplicate scheduling or consumer ambiguity if downstream tooling assumes one canonical representation per task.
Expected fix: Keep one canonical queue entry shape for this task (prefer the structured object with metadata) and remove the duplicate plain-string line.

jury_feedback_history.summary: No blocking contract/state/session-relay regressions were found in this slice; targeted download/install and release-selection tests for the changed areas pass. One queue-contract hygiene issue remains.

blocking_issues.body:
[contracts] queue-overlay-bounded-mirror-drift
`python3 scripts/verify_design_mirror_bundle.py --json` exits non-zero and reports `published_queue_overlay` with `status: queue_drift`, mismatches `mode:prepend` and `source_items`.; Current `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` widens `source_items` to seven files (`NEXT_90_DAY_PRODUCT_ADVANCE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `PUBLIC_LANDING_POLICY.md`, `PUBLIC_DOWNLOADS_POLICY.md`, `PUBLIC_LANDING_MANIFEST.yaml`, `PUBLIC_FEATURE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `horizons/karma-forge.md`, plus queue staging) instead of the bounded two-file local mirror bundle.; The repo’s contract checks expect the overlay to stay bounded: `tests/test_design_mirror_bundle_contracts.py` and `tests/test_operator_contracts.py` both assert the mirror slice uses only `WEEKLY_PRODUCT_PULSE.generated.json` and `NEXT_90_DAY_QUEUE_STAGING.generated.yaml`, and `scripts/verify_design_mirror_bundle.py` enforces the same shape.
Expected fix: Restore `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` to the bounded mirror-drift contract expected by the verifier and tests: keep the single `audit-task-4257456` slice, set the queue mode to the repo’s expected mode, and reduce `source_items` back to the two local design-mirror bundle files.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The reviewed slice currently violates its own bounded design-mirror queue contract: `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` has drifted to `mode: prepend` with widened `source_items`, so the repo’s mirror-bundle verifier fails even though the mirrored product files themselves are in parity.

blocking_issues.body:
[review] slice-scope-drift-mixed-branch
`git diff --name-only main...HEAD` spans 154 files across `.codex-design/`, `.codex-studio/`, `docs/`, `scripts/`, `src/`, `tests/`, `feedback/`, and `logs/`, so this is not a bounded single-slice review surface.; The prompt is internally inconsistent: `Current slice` names the Dense/U4-U5 queue item, while the compact review packet title names the Catalog/U7-U8 queue item.; Representative non-slice files in the diff include adapter code, token canon changes, preview/catalog changes, verify script changes, test harness changes, and many snapshot fixtures, so visual/accessibility/test findings cannot be attributed safely to one authoritative slice.; This exact blocker is already recorded in `feedback/2026-04-21-github-review-pr.md` and `feedback/2026-04-22-github-review-pr.md`, and current branch state still matches that mixed-branch condition.; `AGENTS.md` requires `Keep changes scoped to the queue item.`
Expected fix: Restack or split the branch so it contains only the authoritative queue slice under review, or explicitly widen and reconcile the slice definition and review packet so the changed files match that scope before re-requesting review.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The branch is not reviewable as the requested single queue slice because `main...HEAD` contains a mixed multi-slice diff and the prompt itself names conflicting slice scopes.

blocking_issues.body:
[contracts] contracts-quarantine-still-exports-upstream-dtos
`MediaFactoryContracts.cs` still publicly exports upstream-authoring DTOs such as `PacketFactoryRequest`, `PacketAttachment*`, and `RouteCinema*` with fields like `Subject`, `Attachments`, `WaypointScript`, `TravelSummary`, and `ReviewState` ([lines 149-223]).; The guardrail script now accepts those mixed DTOs as long as they carry an `EXTRACT-008A quarantine` obsolete marker instead of failing on their continued presence ([scripts/ai/contract-boundary-tests.sh:68-90]).; This still violates the repo review rule to keep DTOs render-only rather than merely quarantining upstream semantics inside the public media contract package.
Expected fix: Remove these mixed compatibility DTOs from the public `Chummer.Media.Contracts` surface or move them behind the upstream owner seam, and make the boundary test fail on their presence rather than permitting quarantine markers.

[tests] runtime-verify-missing-reject-lifecycle-coverage
The verifier exercises approval, pin/persist, backup/restore, cache expiry, and job expiry, but it never drives an approval-gated asset through `Rejected` state ([Program.cs:7-108]).; `AGENTS.md` and `.codex-design/review/REVIEW_CONTEXT.md` require lifecycle coverage for approval/persist/reject, yet the executable regression path only proves the approve/persist branch.; Because `scripts/ai/verify.sh` relies on this runtime verifier, the reject path can regress without any executable signal.
Expected fix: Add a reject-path verification case that rejects an approval-gated asset and asserts rejected retention state, rejection timestamp behavior, no persisted/pinned carryover, and correct restore behavior after backup/restore.

[review] milestone-m8-completion-truth-still-overstates-dto-closure
HEAD marks `M8 finished media plant` as `complete` based on `DS-01`..`DS-05` alone ([IMPLEMENTATION_SCOPE.md lines 120-127 in HEAD]).; The DTO execution evidence explicitly says the generic DTO prompt is not fully closed by `DS-01`..`DS-05`, inventories remaining compatibility residue, and maps follow-on work to `DS-06`..`DS-09` ([docs/EXTRACT-008-DS-execution-evidence.md:239-305]).; `WORKLIST.md` simultaneously keeps `EXTRACT-008A` queued because the compatibility DTO residue is still open ([WORKLIST.md:29-30]).
Expected fix: Update milestone coverage/completion truth so M8 and the DTO-boundary lane reflect the still-open `EXTRACT-008A` compatibility residue instead of claiming full closure from `DS-01`..`DS-05`.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The branch still carries the previously reported render-boundary leakage and lifecycle/test gaps: compatibility DTOs remain publicly exported under quarantine markers, reject-path lifecycle coverage is still missing from runtime verification, and milestone truth still overstates M8/DTO closure despite the queued EXTRACT-008A residue.

blocking_issues.body:
[tests] tests-hardcoded-checkout-path
`tests/test_sync_public_guide_from_design.py:9` sets `MODULE_PATH = Path("/docker/chummercomplete/Chummer6/scripts/sync_public_guide_from_design.py")`.; The previous test imported `scripts.sync_public_guide_from_design` normally from the repo; this change makes the suite depend on one specific filesystem location and will fail in any other checkout or CI workspace where that absolute path does not exist.
Expected fix: Load the script module via a repo-relative path or restore the normal import so the tests run from any checkout location, then keep the new manifest normalization assertions on top of that portable import path.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The Windows-path normalization fix looks correct, but the new test harness regresses portability by hard-coding this machine's checkout path, so the added coverage is not reliable outside this workspace.

The targeted unit test suite passes locally with no new actionable regressions or missing-test gaps found in this slice.

blocking_issues.body:
[tests] missing-test-dot-prefixed-generated-from
`_render_manifest()` now explicitly drops `.` path segments (`part != "."`) at `scripts/sync_public_guide_from_design.py:199-203`, which is the substantive behavior change in this slice.; The added test at `tests/test_sync_public_guide_from_design.py:108-122` only covers an already-clean repo-relative path (`products/chummer/...`), not the newly handled `./products/chummer/...` or `.\\products\\chummer\\...` forms.; That leaves the exact feedback fix unguarded, so a later refactor could regress the new normalization path without any test failure.
Expected fix: Add a focused test that exercises dot-prefixed repo-relative `generated_from` values (POSIX and/or Windows form) and asserts they normalize to `products/chummer/...`.

non_blocking_issues.body:
[review] test-import-now-relies-on-namespace-package-resolution
The test previously loaded the target module from its exact file path; it now does `from scripts import sync_public_guide_from_design as guide_sync` at `tests/test_sync_public_guide_from_design.py:13`.; This works here because `sys.path` is adjusted, but `scripts` is not a regular package in this repo (`scripts/__init__.py` is absent), so the import now depends on namespace-package resolution for a very generic top-level name.
Expected fix: Prefer the previous file-path-based import, or make `scripts` an explicit package before relying on package import semantics in tests.

[contracts] manifest-assets-contract-drift
`_render_manifest()` now overwrites `manifest["assets"]` from `src.parent / "assets"` instead of preserving the source manifest payload.; The upstream source bundle at `/docker/chummercomplete/chummer-design/products/chummer/public-guide/manifest.generated.json` lists only PNG asset paths, but the synced repo manifest now advertises additional `.avif` and `.webp` files not present in the source manifest.; That means `--check` no longer validates repo manifest content against the source manifest contract; it validates against a locally recomputed file list instead. The new test `test_assets_are_derived_from_bundle_contents` locks in that drift.
Expected fix: Keep `manifest.generated.json` authoritative from the source bundle and only normalize `generated_from`; remove the filesystem-derived `assets` rewrite and replace the test with one that preserves the source manifest asset list.

[tests] tests-direct-exec-import-regression
The only local change replaces the repo-relative `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` loader with `from scripts import sync_public_guide_from_design as guide_sync`.; `python -m unittest tests.test_sync_public_guide_from_design -v` passes from the repo root, but `python tests/test_sync_public_guide_from_design.py` now fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'scripts'` because direct execution sets `sys.path[0]` to `tests/`, not the repo root.; This is a regression from the previous repo-relative loader approach, which did not depend on the caller already having the repo root on `sys.path`.
Expected fix: Restore a repo-relative module load that works when the test file is executed directly, or otherwise add the repo root to `sys.path` before importing `scripts.sync_public_guide_from_design`.

blocking_issues.body:
[review] slice-status-plane-verifier-missing
Slice goal is STATUS_PLANE drift verifier coverage, but the only diff vs `main` is `controller/app.py` (account model/backoff logic).; No verifier/test artifact changes were made for `STATUS_PLANE.generated.yaml` readiness/deployment semantics.
Expected fix: Implement the requested STATUS_PLANE verifier coverage (and corresponding tests) that fails on drift from live readiness/deployment semantics.

[correctness] model-capability-fallback-regression
`effective_allowed_models_for_account` now returns `allowed_models` on no-overlap instead of capability-derived models.; Call sites (e.g. account/model selection and local review model selection) use that result directly and do not enforce capability membership afterward, so stale/configured `allowed_models` can select models not in live capability probes.
Expected fix: Reintroduce capability-safe fallback behavior (or explicit post-check against capability models) so selected models cannot bypass live capability constraints; add unit tests for overlap/no-overlap cases across auth kinds.

jury_feedback_history.summary: Current delta is off-slice and introduces untested account-model selection behavior that can drift from live capability semantics.

Reviewed the slice diff against `main` for regressions and missing tests; the new status-plane verifier is wired into Fleet verify, enforces readiness/deployment semantic parity from live admin status, and includes focused pass/fail/missing-artifact coverage with no actionable defects found.

queue.title: Make fleet trust and status publication strictly live-truth so stale aggregate snapshots, poisoned completion receipts, and misleading ETA/currentness states cannot survive into post-client operations.
Extract Chummer-specific shard topology, focus heuristics, proof paths, and owner-group routing out of launcher logic into explicit project configuration contracts.
Add steady-state fleet operating profiles (`maintenance`, `standard`, `burst`) with explicit shard caps, resource budgets, and safe promotion-demotion rules.
Promote executable gates, parity proofs, and flagship-readiness materializers into first-class orchestrated jobs with freshness windows, retries, and dependency edges.
Build a compact operator surface for shard mix, queue health, proof freshness, account health, resource pressure, and blocked milestones.
Consolidate restart-safe fleet runtime configuration and validate reboot recovery behavior so policy survives supervisor restarts and host reboots without tribal knowledge.

supervisor_contract.purpose: Keep Chummer-specific supervisor topology, focus routing, and proof paths in project config instead of launcher-only code or env folklore.

burst.demotion_rule: drop back to standard on warning memory pressure, repeated high-demand provider errors, or queue contraction
burst.description: Aggressive capacity mode for broad closeout/successor waves when memory and credits are healthy.
burst.promotion_rule: enter only when status is unblocked, memory is ok, and active queue breadth exceeds standard capacity

maintenance.demotion_rule: demote here when memory is critical, credits are guarded, or external providers are unstable
maintenance.description: Conservative keepalive mode for proof refresh, unblock checks, and low-pressure maintenance.
maintenance.promotion_rule: promote only when host memory is ok and the operator explicitly selects standard or burst

standard.demotion_rule: demote to maintenance when memory pressure or provider instability persists across OODA passes
standard.description: Default steady-state mode for closeout plus successor-wave work without exhausting host headroom.
standard.promotion_rule: promote to burst only with healthy memory and enough open frontier to keep the wider fleet productive

blocking_issues.body:
[review] mirror-audit-row-not-bounded-to-stale-set
`scripts/ai/verify_design_mirror.py:108-122` only checks that `source_items` is non-empty, rooted under `/.codex-design/`, and free of `..`; it never checks that the queue row actually covers the stale mirror files it is supposed to bound.; Running `python3 scripts/ai/verify_design_mirror.py` on this branch returns `stale_paths=7 queue_errors=0`, with stale files including `.codex-design/product/README.md`, `VISION.md`, `USER_JOURNEYS.md`, and `RELEASE_PIPELINE.md`.; The published queue row at `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml:9-12` lists only `NEXT_90_DAY_PRODUCT_ADVANCE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `NEXT_90_DAY_QUEUE_STAGING.generated.yaml`, and `horizons/karma-forge.md`, so the verifier currently accepts a row that does not describe the full stale bundle.
Expected fix: Tighten the verifier so the single `audit-task-11707` row is validated against the actual stale mirror bundle it is standing in for, or regenerate the row so its bounded scope matches the stale files the verifier detects.

[tests] missing-stale-row-regression-tests
`Chummer.CoreEngine.Tests/Program.cs:5980-6045` only covers the clean-mirror/no-row case and the unexpected-leftover-file failure case.; There is no test that proves the intended new contract for this slice: stale mirror bytes plus exactly one bounded `audit-task-11707` queue row should be the only acceptable in-progress state.; There is also no test for the repeat-churn failure modes this change is meant to prevent, such as duplicate `audit-task-11707` rows or a malformed bounded row that the verifier should reject.
Expected fix: Add regression tests that exercise `verify_design_mirror.py` for the stale-with-single-row success path and for duplicate/misaligned queue-row failures.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The mirror-drift guard is close, but it does not actually enforce that the published `audit-task-11707` queue slice matches the stale mirror bundle, and the new verifier path is missing regression coverage for the intended stale-with-one-bounded-row behavior.

blocking_issues.body:
[review] review-env-shell-blocked
Every attempted read command failed before execution with: "bwrap: No permissions to create a new namespace...".; Unable to load required review inputs or run branch diff against main, so contract/compatibility/state checks were not performable.
Expected fix: Restore read-only shell/file access (or provide equivalent file and diff artifacts) and rerun round 1 review.

jury_feedback_history.summary: Local fallback review could not be executed because the runtime cannot read repository files or git state due a sandbox namespace failure.

non_blocking_issues.body:
[contracts] queue-duplicate-mixed-entry-11709
The same design-mirror task appears twice: once as a plain string entry and again as a structured object (`title/task/package_id`) in the same `items` list.; This introduces mixed entry shapes for one logical task and can lead to duplicate scheduling or consumer ambiguity if downstream tooling assumes one canonical representation per task.
Expected fix: Keep one canonical queue entry shape for this task (prefer the structured object with metadata) and remove the duplicate plain-string line.

jury_feedback_history.summary: No blocking contract/state/session-relay regressions were found in this slice; targeted download/install and release-selection tests for the changed areas pass. One queue-contract hygiene issue remains.

blocking_issues.body:
[contracts] queue-overlay-bounded-mirror-drift
`python3 scripts/verify_design_mirror_bundle.py --json` exits non-zero and reports `published_queue_overlay` with `status: queue_drift`, mismatches `mode:prepend` and `source_items`.; Current `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` widens `source_items` to seven files (`NEXT_90_DAY_PRODUCT_ADVANCE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `PUBLIC_LANDING_POLICY.md`, `PUBLIC_DOWNLOADS_POLICY.md`, `PUBLIC_LANDING_MANIFEST.yaml`, `PUBLIC_FEATURE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `horizons/karma-forge.md`, plus queue staging) instead of the bounded two-file local mirror bundle.; The repo’s contract checks expect the overlay to stay bounded: `tests/test_design_mirror_bundle_contracts.py` and `tests/test_operator_contracts.py` both assert the mirror slice uses only `WEEKLY_PRODUCT_PULSE.generated.json` and `NEXT_90_DAY_QUEUE_STAGING.generated.yaml`, and `scripts/verify_design_mirror_bundle.py` enforces the same shape.
Expected fix: Restore `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` to the bounded mirror-drift contract expected by the verifier and tests: keep the single `audit-task-4257456` slice, set the queue mode to the repo’s expected mode, and reduce `source_items` back to the two local design-mirror bundle files.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The reviewed slice currently violates its own bounded design-mirror queue contract: `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` has drifted to `mode: prepend` with widened `source_items`, so the repo’s mirror-bundle verifier fails even though the mirrored product files themselves are in parity.

blocking_issues.body:
[review] slice-scope-drift-mixed-branch
`git diff --name-only main...HEAD` spans 154 files across `.codex-design/`, `.codex-studio/`, `docs/`, `scripts/`, `src/`, `tests/`, `feedback/`, and `logs/`, so this is not a bounded single-slice review surface.; The prompt is internally inconsistent: `Current slice` names the Dense/U4-U5 queue item, while the compact review packet title names the Catalog/U7-U8 queue item.; Representative non-slice files in the diff include adapter code, token canon changes, preview/catalog changes, verify script changes, test harness changes, and many snapshot fixtures, so visual/accessibility/test findings cannot be attributed safely to one authoritative slice.; This exact blocker is already recorded in `feedback/2026-04-21-github-review-pr.md` and `feedback/2026-04-22-github-review-pr.md`, and current branch state still matches that mixed-branch condition.; `AGENTS.md` requires `Keep changes scoped to the queue item.`
Expected fix: Restack or split the branch so it contains only the authoritative queue slice under review, or explicitly widen and reconcile the slice definition and review packet so the changed files match that scope before re-requesting review.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The branch is not reviewable as the requested single queue slice because `main...HEAD` contains a mixed multi-slice diff and the prompt itself names conflicting slice scopes.

[review] slice-scope-drift-mixed-branch
`git diff --name-only main...HEAD` spans 154 files across `.codex-design/`, `.codex-studio/`, `docs/`, `scripts/`, `src/`, `tests/`, `feedback/`, and `logs/`, so this is not a bounded single-slice review surface.; The prompt is internally inconsistent: `Current slice` names the Dense/U4-U5 queue item, while the compact review packet title names the Catalog/U7-U8 queue item.; Representative non-slice files in the diff include adapter code, token canon changes, preview/catalog changes, verify script changes, test harness changes, and many snapshot fixtures, so visual/accessibility/test findings cannot be attributed safely to one authoritative slice.; This exact blocker is already recorded in `feedback/2026-04-21-github-review-pr.md` and `feedback/2026-04-22-github-review-pr.md`, and current branch state still matches that mixed-branch condition.; `AGENTS.md` requires `Keep changes scoped to the queue item.`
Expected fix: Restack or split the branch so it contains only the authoritative queue slice under review, or explicitly widen and reconcile the slice definition and review packet so the changed files match that scope before re-requesting review.

last_review_feedback.summary: The branch is not reviewable as the requested single queue slice because `main...HEAD` contains a mixed multi-slice diff and the prompt itself names conflicting slice scopes.

blocking_issues.body:
[contracts] contracts-quarantine-still-exports-upstream-dtos
`MediaFactoryContracts.cs` still publicly exports upstream-authoring DTOs such as `PacketFactoryRequest`, `PacketAttachment*`, and `RouteCinema*` with fields like `Subject`, `Attachments`, `WaypointScript`, `TravelSummary`, and `ReviewState` ([lines 149-223]).; The guardrail script now accepts those mixed DTOs as long as they carry an `EXTRACT-008A quarantine` obsolete marker instead of failing on their continued presence ([scripts/ai/contract-boundary-tests.sh:68-90]).; This still violates the repo review rule to keep DTOs render-only rather than merely quarantining upstream semantics inside the public media contract package.
Expected fix: Remove these mixed compatibility DTOs from the public `Chummer.Media.Contracts` surface or move them behind the upstream owner seam, and make the boundary test fail on their presence rather than permitting quarantine markers.

[tests] runtime-verify-missing-reject-lifecycle-coverage
The verifier exercises approval, pin/persist, backup/restore, cache expiry, and job expiry, but it never drives an approval-gated asset through `Rejected` state ([Program.cs:7-108]).; `AGENTS.md` and `.codex-design/review/REVIEW_CONTEXT.md` require lifecycle coverage for approval/persist/reject, yet the executable regression path only proves the approve/persist branch.; Because `scripts/ai/verify.sh` relies on this runtime verifier, the reject path can regress without any executable signal.
Expected fix: Add a reject-path verification case that rejects an approval-gated asset and asserts rejected retention state, rejection timestamp behavior, no persisted/pinned carryover, and correct restore behavior after backup/restore.

[review] milestone-m8-completion-truth-still-overstates-dto-closure
HEAD marks `M8 finished media plant` as `complete` based on `DS-01`..`DS-05` alone ([IMPLEMENTATION_SCOPE.md lines 120-127 in HEAD]).; The DTO execution evidence explicitly says the generic DTO prompt is not fully closed by `DS-01`..`DS-05`, inventories remaining compatibility residue, and maps follow-on work to `DS-06`..`DS-09` ([docs/EXTRACT-008-DS-execution-evidence.md:239-305]).; `WORKLIST.md` simultaneously keeps `EXTRACT-008A` queued because the compatibility DTO residue is still open ([WORKLIST.md:29-30]).
Expected fix: Update milestone coverage/completion truth so M8 and the DTO-boundary lane reflect the still-open `EXTRACT-008A` compatibility residue instead of claiming full closure from `DS-01`..`DS-05`.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The branch still carries the previously reported render-boundary leakage and lifecycle/test gaps: compatibility DTOs remain publicly exported under quarantine markers, reject-path lifecycle coverage is still missing from runtime verification, and milestone truth still overstates M8/DTO closure despite the queued EXTRACT-008A residue.

blocking_issues.body:
[tests] tests-hardcoded-checkout-path
`tests/test_sync_public_guide_from_design.py:9` sets `MODULE_PATH = Path("/docker/chummercomplete/Chummer6/scripts/sync_public_guide_from_design.py")`.; The previous test imported `scripts.sync_public_guide_from_design` normally from the repo; this change makes the suite depend on one specific filesystem location and will fail in any other checkout or CI workspace where that absolute path does not exist.
Expected fix: Load the script module via a repo-relative path or restore the normal import so the tests run from any checkout location, then keep the new manifest normalization assertions on top of that portable import path.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The Windows-path normalization fix looks correct, but the new test harness regresses portability by hard-coding this machine's checkout path, so the added coverage is not reliable outside this workspace.

No actionable regressions or missing-test gaps found in this slice. The test import change is covered by the targeted test module passing, and the added package marker is consistent with the new import path.

Working tree changes address the two review notes without introducing an actionable regression. The test import is now checkout-portable, the Windows-path normalization case is covered, and the targeted test suite passes.

No actionable regressions or missing-test gaps found in the current working-tree slice. The test import is portable again, the Windows-path manifest case remains covered, and the targeted unittest suite passes.

The feedback fixes are in place: `_render_manifest()` now normalizes Windows-style separators before extracting the repo-relative `products/chummer/...` path, and the test import no longer hardcodes a checkout-specific absolute path. The targeted unit test suite passes locally with no new actionable regressions or missing-test gaps found in this slice.

Reviewed the working tree diff against `main` for the manifest normalization feedback fix and portable test import update. No actionable regressions, contract drift, state-safety issues, or missing blocking tests were found in this slice.

The working-tree changes correctly address the Windows-path normalization feedback in `_render_manifest()` and add direct test coverage for that portability case; I did not find actionable regressions, contract drift, state-safety issues, or missing tests in this review slice.

The local worktree addresses both recorded feedback items: manifest path normalization now handles Windows separators and the test import path is repo-relative again. I did not find actionable regressions, contract drift, state-safety issues, or missing follow-up tests in this slice.

blocking_issues.body:
[tests] missing-test-dot-prefixed-generated-from
`_render_manifest()` now explicitly drops `.` path segments (`part != "."`) at `scripts/sync_public_guide_from_design.py:199-203`, which is the substantive behavior change in this slice.; The added test at `tests/test_sync_public_guide_from_design.py:108-122` only covers an already-clean repo-relative path (`products/chummer/...`), not the newly handled `./products/chummer/...` or `.\\products\\chummer\\...` forms.; That leaves the exact feedback fix unguarded, so a later refactor could regress the new normalization path without any test failure.
Expected fix: Add a focused test that exercises dot-prefixed repo-relative `generated_from` values (POSIX and/or Windows form) and asserts they normalize to `products/chummer/...`.

non_blocking_issues.body:
[review] test-import-now-relies-on-namespace-package-resolution
The test previously loaded the target module from its exact file path; it now does `from scripts import sync_public_guide_from_design as guide_sync` at `tests/test_sync_public_guide_from_design.py:13`.; This works here because `sys.path` is adjusted, but `scripts` is not a regular package in this repo (`scripts/__init__.py` is absent), so the import now depends on namespace-package resolution for a very generic top-level name.
Expected fix: Prefer the previous file-path-based import, or make `scripts` an explicit package before relying on package import semantics in tests.

blocking_issues.body:
[tests] tests-direct-exec-import-regression
The only local change replaces the repo-relative `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` loader with `from scripts import sync_public_guide_from_design as guide_sync`.; `python -m unittest tests.test_sync_public_guide_from_design -v` passes from the repo root, but `python tests/test_sync_public_guide_from_design.py` now fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'scripts'` because direct execution sets `sys.path[0]` to `tests/`, not the repo root.; This is a regression from the previous repo-relative loader approach, which did not depend on the caller already having the repo root on `sys.path`.
Expected fix: Restore a repo-relative module load that works when the test file is executed directly, or otherwise add the repo root to `sys.path` before importing `scripts.sync_public_guide_from_design`.

notes: Deterministic enforced capacity plan. Quartermaster compiles desired capacity while fleet-controller remains the sole runtime dispatcher and reconciler.
Controller tick driver is controller_tick; lane families stay static while Fleet reconciles dynamic worker and lease instances.
1min telemetry is expected from ea_onemin_manager via the ea manager (manager), not direct BrowserAct runtime orchestration.
Booster admission requires capacity, useful work, and non-conflicting package scope; scope leases are treated as a first-class cap.
Studio remains intentionally serial; this plan governs booster, review, and audit pools below the design plane.
